sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच।
मया प्रसन्नेन तवार्जुनेदं
रूपं परं दर्शितमात्मयोगात्।
तेजोमयं विश्वमनन्तमाद्यं
यन्मे त्वदन्येन न दृष्टपूर्वम्॥
transliteration:
śrī-bhagavān uvāca
mayā prasannena tavārjunedaṁ
rūpaṁ paraṁ darśitamātma-yogāt
tejo-mayaṁ viśvam anantam ādyaṁ
yan me tvad-anyena na dṛṣṭa-pūrvam
literal_meaning: By Me, with delight, to you, O Arjuna, this supreme form has been revealed through My own Yoga—effulgent, all-pervading, endless, primordial—which no one other than you has ever seen before.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reassures Arjuna that the terrifying vision is a rare, grace-bestowed glimpse of ultimate reality, validating Arjuna’s unique worthiness while containing the overwhelming experience within a benevolent frame.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic validation: the psyche is told ‘you are not mad; what you saw is real, sacred, and given only to you,’ converting raw terror into numinous awe.
trigger_scenario: After a panic attack or psychedelic trip that shattered your worldview, a trusted therapist says: ‘What you encountered is rare, real, and meaningful—you were shown something others never see.’
anchor_text: God’s boundless form is shown only to those He dearly loves.
